name: The White House
subject: Dr. King’s legacy, 50 years later
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=63842f4bb3c2c290b959d69ef7218238a21617980aef8f788b46c166e185a786e0e6138ae76ad47e68ca7610aea7c63c385ee7d591bd8b47

Dr. King’s legacy, 50 years later

April 4 marks the kind of anniversary America wishes it never had to endure. Fifty years ago today, the life of our Nation’s greatest civil rights icon was cut short by an assassin’s bullet at the Lorraine Motel in Memphis, Tennessee.

Martin Luther King, Jr., understood that for people to see one another as equals, they had to feel the tugs of a bond stronger than race. For King, that bond was America. He knew that the struggle for civil rights was not about rejecting America’s Founding ideals. On the contrary, it was about living up to our shared values.

“The Reverend’s devotion to fighting the injustice of segregation and discrimination ignited the American spirit of fraternity and reminded us of our higher purpose,” President Donald J. Trump wrote in his 2018 Proclamation celebrating Martin Luther King, Jr., Day earlier this year.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=63842f4bb3c2c290a8584451861e621fb64ac18f9ffa709037d203bdce0547f3ebc70807b380a5d4c360f8ca3fefd3e1e0e0b226171978da
Read more about why Martin Luther King, Jr., is the true model of an American patriot.
More:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=63842f4bb3c2c290565cbe7ef369e6fb5fb4b9b98bf41bd787ee2404476787bb1a7ef9dc92417fd2010551857b7c932fc4090ff9678ac474
President Trump proclaims the 50th anniversary of Dr. King’s assassination

A missed opportunity on immigration

President Trump made it clear this week that Congressional Democrats have been missing in action when it comes to striking an immigration deal on Capitol Hill. As a result, there is no fix in sight for the troubled DACA program.

That outcome is disappointing given clear public support for President Trump’s proposed immigration compromise. The President’s plan has four pillars, each endorsed by a majority of voters:

- 63 percent of voters favor a deal that gives DACA immigrants a path to citizenship in exchange for comprehensive reforms to America’s immigration system and border security
- 84 percent think immigration priority should be based on a person’s ability to contribute to the United States, not based on having relatives here
- 70 percent oppose the Visa lottery system that randomly picks 50,000 people to enter the U.S. each year
- 62 percent believe current border security is inadequate—including 55 percent of Hispanic voters

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=63842f4bb3c2c2901e2204bfc346d42d568052e8d151cd05214daca03794d354686f3f897228a1bf5eeac26e596ad263177afd65d25c1dc5
Learn more about why Democrats have missed a major opportunity on comprehensive immigration reform.

America’s Baltic friends

America’s Baltic allies are crucial strategic partners. Yesterday, President Trump welcomed leaders from NATO members Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania to the White House, congratulating the three countries on the 100th anniversary of their declared independence from Russia.

From the very beginning of Baltic independence, President Trump noted, the United States never ceased to recognize the sovereignty of these three nations. That remained true even through decades of Soviet occupation in Eastern Europe during the Cold War.

The United States continues to stand by its Baltic NATO allies under the Trump Administration. America has boosted defense and security cooperation in the region, helped advanced Baltic energy security, and enriched cultural ties through exchanges and other programs.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=63842f4bb3c2c290bf94c4b38bd62a9d9248d307927ec22eeb3fe5f923a68b2850cf81aab7161d6e6e342b9597c0ea3dac3e8a3f1730d172
Read more about the 3 key ways President Trump is supporting our Baltic NATO allies.

Last night was a loss. There’s no way around that. Conservatives got beat, and unless we get our act together, November isn’t going to be pretty in Wisconsin.

I am sounding the alarm, and so is Governor Walker.

[](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=5e4e6bcf7d524ba899eb8d8163a87855&_e=jt2oIl2_wvDM84ZanOJQJKFeG9Phmm87uT2ZNGez4h-3YrOytlN0cfWsNcHxBR9p6vZgXMsB_pTRe6yUmxscN5JbgE3dQpYoaGGsmbfTROnEMbyDFDE8yfbB2705PSBykfS-e69jBTnf9CDejk-_pTFrmSVKIuKGunLT5CSAahboO53-cnPpcwMZTdObMhtP4HPKNNCmDMTdopyT6CrGOJo4oBxv63C6cbxXZkLWqJPIYUNS-ZUIEATbwkncRgNB2A9tZtk1RgjF0OCVJwv_uTMO4b8jb5HAh_TaD6ZUKvQxBo55xIMOuPLeeM3682nS0uLwd6A5EXE-LMZ5PT9GKDLESUanwjTXEIqRdKggBLZe4F4aKu_5Y2LlEOZfRmH-qp5uqbuGJCFGAQBf0WPztFbePb2wBCLrbtKlyGMgY48%3D)

It's clear that Pruitt would rather cut deals with his friends in the fossil fuel industry and waste taxpayer dollars than protect our environment.
---
Derek --

It's long past time for Scott Pruitt to step down as Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency.

Pruitt has made backdoor deals with energy lobbyists while literally living under their roof -- he took a sweetheart deal from the wife of an energy lobbyist for a luxury condo less than a block from the Capitol in D.C., and then the EPA ruled in favor of one of that lobbyist's clients. Plus Pruitt's EPA looked into renting a private plane at the price tag of $100,000 a month and letting taxpayers foot the bill.

It's clear that Pruitt would rather cut deals with his friends in the fossil fuel industry and use taxpayer dollars for his personal benefit rather than protect our environment. It's time for him to resign.

**Add your name if you agree that Scott Pruitt should step down as head of the EPA.**

**[ADD YOUR NAME](https://my.democrats. "https://my.democrats.org/page/s/call-on-scott-pruitt-to-resign?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+ADD+YOUR+NAME&utm_campaign=em_20180404_xh_xhdems&source=em_20180404_xh_xhdems&refcode=em_20180404_xh_xhdems")**
It shouldn't come as a surprise that someone in Trump's Cabinet doesn't have the background or the ethics to serve our country. Before joining the Trump administration, Scott Pruitt served as attorney general of Oklahoma, where he brought multiple lawsuits against the very agency he now leads. And the news cycle this week has brought to light several instances of Pruitt using taxpayer money inappropriately and accepting unethical assistance from lobbyists.

The decisions Pruitt makes every day impact the planet we share now and will pass on to future generations. In fact, his latest move to roll back greenhouse gas and fuel efficiency standards will not only harm the environment -- it will put Americans' health at risk.

The American people deserve an EPA Administrator who actually cares about protecting our planet for generations to come, which is why Pruitt is the wrong person for the job.

**[It's time for Pruitt to step down from his post. Add your name to call on Scott Pruitt to resign.](https://my.democrats. "https://my.democrats.org/page/s/call-on-scott-pruitt-to-resign?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Its+time+for+Pruitt+to+step+down+from+hi&utm_campaign=em_20180404_xh_xhdems&source=em_20180404_xh_xhdems&refcode=em_20180404_xh_xhdems")**

Thanks for taking a stand against Scott Pruitt. The future of our planet depends on it.

Xochitl

Xochitl Hinojosa
Communications Director

name: Andrew Gillum
subject: 50 years ago
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor
body:
[](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3109/9e00690/45ca3f51/24024865/5079310/VEsH/ "https://andrewgillum.com/")
Friend,

On April 4, 1968, Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. was shot at the Lorraine Motel in Memphis, Tennessee, because he believed and spoke unapologetically for freedom and equality for everyone. He was in Memphis to fight for sanitation workers and jobs with dignity -- and that work goes on today.

We've come a long way towards making Dr. King's dreams a reality -- but we still have a long way to go until everyone is truly equal. And despite everything we've faced, I know if we continue to stand together, we can keep fighting to honor Dr. King's legacy.
Together, we're going to build Florida into a state that creates jobs with dignity, and work that pays a living wage. We're going to invest in quality public education and job training programs, so that more Floridians have the opportunity to break out of the vicious cycle of intergenerational poverty. We're going to transform our state so people can thrive, and not simply just survive.

Together, we're going to heal this state and make freedom and equality a part of everybody's story, no matter what they look like or where they come from. So today, join me in honoring Dr. King and recommitting to his vision of freedom and equality for everyone.

Thank you,

Andrew

Paid for by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor.

---

name: Jason Yates
subject: Another court vacancy you need to know about
body:
Peter,
**On Thursday of last week, United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit Judge Stephen Reinhardt** **passed away.** He was 87. Justice Reinhardt was nominated to the court by former President Jimmy Carter in 1980 and was still active on the court until his death.
Reinhardt was the liberal face of the Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals who was involved in many high-profile decisions over the course of his judicial career. He authored the Ninth Circuit’s 2012 decision on Proposition 8, overturning California’s state ban on gay marriage. He also joined a ruling to remove ‘under God’ from the pledge of allegiance, stating it violated the U.S. Constitution. That ruling was later overturned.
Reinhardt’s passing leaves a new vacancy on the Court of Appeals, which is one of the federal judicial positions filled through a nomination process by the President of the United States.
This made us wonder about the number of current federal judicial vacancies.
Peter, do you know the number of current vacancies for judges to be nominated by President Trump and serve a life term?
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F2018-04-04-federal-judicial-vacancies%2F%3Fa%3D1&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180404qna01&n=1)
[](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2F2018-04-04-federal-judicial-vacancies%2F%3Fa%3D2&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e180404qna01&n=2)

**United. We Stand.**
Jason Yates

body:

Friend,

UNTIL THE WALL IS BUILT, I WILL DO EVERYTHING IN MY POWER AS COMMANDER IN CHIEF TO SECURE THE BORDER.
